pr.title: Split-up improvements across common, GM/MN, TY, and baselib
pr.author: fjooord

summary: Small cross-system split-up matching PR that improved exact and near matches in common fighter bury code, camera, GM/MN menu/game-mode code, TY display, map collision, and sysdolphin/baselib. The PR reported 5 new exact matches, +1608 matched code bytes, +24 matched data bytes, 8 unmatched-item improvements, and no regressions after ninja and the Decomp Orchestrator regression gate.
what_changed: Camera_80030CFC stack layout was adjusted by adding a 12-byte local pad and reducing PAD_STACK from 14 to 2, producing a reported exact match for main/melee/cm/camera Camera_80030CFC.

ftCo_Bury.c gained force-active .sdata2 literals ftCo_804D8C28, ftCo_804D8C30, ftCo_804D8C38, and ftCo_804D8C3C, and removed an unused zeroed 8-byte local in ftCo_800C0D0C. The PR notes these constants correspond to existing symbols.txt entries and the split-owned .sdata2 range.

gm_1832.c added PAD_STACK(4) in fn_8018325C and rewrote fn_801884F8 to access lbl_80473700 through an int pointer with ptr[65] and ptr[66] instead of named result_cache fields, matching local codegen.
gmregclear.c and gmstaffroll.c made narrow local declaration ordering changes in fn_8017F2A4 and gm_801AC6D8_OnEnter.
mncount.c wrapped mnCount_8025092C's large local working set in an inner block and added PAD_STACK(4), preserving the rank/tie handling logic while reshaping stack/local lifetimes.
mnitemsw.c removed an unused sp40 local and introduced u8 arg1_ and arg2_ copies before testing the arguments in mnItemSw_8023453C.
mnmain.c changed one mn_8022A440 call argument from mn_804A04F0.prev_menu to data->hovered_selection and reordered locals in mn_8022B3A0, including moving StaticModelDesc* top earlier.
mplib.c changed mpCheckFloor distance calculations to reuse a single dist2 local and SQ(px_sp54 - ax) + SQ(py_sp50 - ay), with a small pad local added inside the loop.
tydisplay.c adjusted local ordering and split entry->x04 handling into c2 and cat in un_8031BC54, using c2 for archive indexing and cat for signed category handling.

axdriver.c corrected/rearranged intermediate stereo-volume expressions in AXDriver_8038BF6C, changing tmp2 to left_inv_sqrt * right_inv_sqrt and passing right_sqrt * left_inv_sqrt in the later argument.
particle.c changed hsd_80393DA0 to clear hsd_804CF7E8 except for the x24 member before assigning out_buf and buf_size.
smart_moves: Kept the PR narrow despite touching many systems: most changes are local stack-shape, declaration-order, temporary-variable, or expression-shape edits tied to regression results.
Used force_active only for the ftCo_Bury .sdata2 literals that the PR says were already represented in symbols.txt and owned by the ftCo_Bury split range, avoiding broad data churn.
Matched gm_1832 codegen by using pointer indexing into lbl_80473700, explicitly mirroring nearby pointer-style access in fn_8018846C according to the PR notes.
Preserved source-literal and HSD_ASSERT form in tydisplay for the earlier un_8031B1FC reshaping attempt; the PR notes that older string-base/assert reshaping was dropped.
Used an inner scope in mnCount_8025092C to influence stack/local lifetimes without rewriting the sorting and tie-ranking algorithm.
For mpCheckFloor, collapsed repeated dx/dy/dx2/dy2 temporaries into SQ() expressions while retaining a shared dist2 local, a compact way to affect codegen without changing the geometric test.
decomp_lessons: For exact matching, local declaration order, artificial pads, and inner scopes can matter as much as semantic C logic, especially in functions with large stack frames such as mnCount_8025092C and Camera_80030CFC.

When a split owns a .sdata2 range, adding force-active constants with existing symbol names can resolve data matching cleanly, but the PR evidence supports this only for ftCo_Bury's specific constants.
Named struct-field access is not always codegen-equivalent to raw pointer indexing; fn_801884F8 matched by indexing lbl_80473700 as int* with known offsets.
Argument shadow copies such as u8 arg1_ and arg2_ can be useful when matching promotion/storage behavior in menu routines.
Use nearby already-matching functions as style/codegen guides; the PR explicitly cites mirroring pointer-style access from a nearby gm_1832 function.
Avoid overfitting risky source rewrites when they are not needed: the PR notes the tydisplay string-base/assert reshaping was removed in favor of keeping original string literals and HSD_ASSERT form.
Partial memset size adjustments can be required for baselib state structs when a member should survive or be initialized separately, as in hsd_80393DA0 excluding x24 from the initial clear.
naming_conventions: Kept existing decomp symbol style for unknown or address-derived functions: Camera_80030CFC, ftCo_800C0D0C, fn_801884F8, mnCount_8025092C, mnItemSw_8023453C, hsd_80393DA0.
Force-active data symbols used file/module prefix plus address: ftCo_804D8C28, ftCo_804D8C30, ftCo_804D8C38, ftCo_804D8C3C.

Temporary match-shaping locals used terse names tied to existing variable purpose, such as c2 for the raw TY category/archive index, arg1_ and arg2_ for shadowed u8 parameters, ptr for raw lbl_80473700 indexing, and dist2 for squared distance.
Stack padding followed existing project idioms: PAD_STACK(n), explicit u8 _PAD[12], and local pad arrays.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added PAD_STACK calls and explicit padding locals to alter stack frame size/layout.
Reordered local declarations to influence register allocation and stack slot placement.
Introduced an inner block in mnCount_8025092C to narrow local lifetime and affect stack layout.
Used raw int* pointer indexing into a global structure to match stores/loads at specific offsets.
Added #pragma force_active on/reset around split-owned constants to force .sdata2 emission.
Replaced expanded dx/dy squared-distance temporaries with SQ() macro expressions assigned to a reused local.
Introduced u8 parameter shadow copies to match argument handling in mnItemSw_8023453C.
Changed memset size from sizeof(struct) to sizeof(struct) - sizeof(member) to match initialization behavior/codegen.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided excerpt.
PR body reviewer notes state that an earlier tydisplay string-base/assert reshaping for un_8031B1FC was dropped, keeping source string literals and HSD_ASSERT form.
PR body reviewer notes justify ftCo_Bury force-active .sdata2 literals as corresponding to existing symbols.txt entries and the ftCo_Bury.c split-owned .sdata2 range.
PR body reviewer notes justify gm_1832 pointer indexing in fn_801884F8 as mirroring nearby pointer-style access in fn_8018846C.
follow_up_queries: Look for other gm_1832 functions near fn_8018846C/fn_801884F8 where struct-field access may need pointer indexing to match.
Search for remaining split-owned .sdata2 ranges with existing symbols.txt entries that may need tightly scoped force_active constants.
Review unmatched MN menu functions for stack-shaping opportunities using inner scopes, u8 argument shadow copies, and declaration-order changes.
Audit baselib memset initializers where a trailing or embedded member should be excluded from the clear and initialized separately.
Check other mp collision routines for SQ() expression shaping and shared distance locals similar to mpCheckFloor.
